terms.definition: Using mean (and std) of fold validation metrics to choose among competing models or feature sets on limited data—reduces overfitting to one random validation partition.
CV run on an outer training pool solely to pick hyperparameters or model type; its best score must not be reported as final generalization.
CV loop that holds out data never used for tuning in that round; mean outer scores estimate generalization after nested selection.
Inflated performance when many hyperparameter settings are ranked on the same validation folds and the best inner CV score is treated as unbiased.
scikit-learn helper that runs an inner CV loop over a parameter grid and refits the best estimator on all training data passed to fit.
Low standard deviation of per-fold validation metrics—often preferred over a slightly higher mean with high spread when mitigating overfit-prone models.
